results.title: HUMAN TUMOR-ASSOCIATED THOMSEN-FRIEDENREICH ANTIGEN
results.abstract: Human tumor associated Thomsen-Friedenreich (TF) antigen is purified from adenocarcinoma conditioned media, adenocarcinoma cell detergent extracts or plural effusion fluid by affinity chromatography using an insolubilized TF-specific monoclonal antibody, MAb 49H.8. The TF antigen is a glycoprotein c

results.title: Human C-FMS antigen binding proteins
results.abstract: Antigen binding proteins that bind to human c-fms protein are provided. Nucleic acids encoding the antigen binding protein, vectors, and cells encoding the same are also provided. The antigen binding proteins can inhibit binding of c-fms to CSF-1, reduce monocyte migration into tumors, and reduce th

results.title: Peptides and combination of peptides for use in immunotherapy against prostate cancer and other cancers
results.abstract: The present invention relates to peptides, proteins, nucleic acids and cells for use in immunotherapeutic methods. In particular, the present invention relates to the immunotherapy of cancer. The present invention furthermore relates to tumor associated T-cell peptide epitopes, alone or in combinati

results.title: Methods for enhancing an immune response from a 43 KD human cancer antigen
results.abstract: A tumor associated protein has been discovered to exhibit immunoreactivity to human monoclonal antibody L92. A ten amino acid peptide segment of the protein and a fourteen amino acid peptide which contains the ten amino acid peptide has also been isolated and exhibits immunoreactivity to the same an

results.title: Restoration of tumor suppression using MRNA-based delivery system
results.abstract: Compositions and methods for treating cancer that include administering a therapeutically effective amount of a tumor suppressor mRNA complexed with a delivery vehicle as described herein, e.g., a nanoparticle.

results.title: New combination therapy and its use
results.abstract: This invention relates to combination therapy for the treatment of cancer, and methods for its use. The combination therapy is an antibody that binds (a) to CD137, or its antigen binding fragments and (b) more immunotherapy agents, including further immunotherapy agents, which are PD-1 in tors. This

results.title: Membrane anchor/active compound conjugate, its preparation and its uses
results.abstract: According to certain embodiments, the invention relates to an immunoconjugate produced by conjugating at least one membrane-anchoring compound with at least one partial sequence of a protein of a virus, a bacterium, a parasite or a tumor antigen. results.title: ANTIBODIES BINDING IGC2 OF IGSF11 (VSIG3) AND USES THEREOF
results.abstract: The present invention is based on the surprising discovery that antibodies that bind to the immunoglobulin-like (Ig) domain of the extracellular domain (ECD) of IGSF11 (VSIG3) can also inhibit the interaction between IGSF11 and IGSF11 receptors, such as VSIR (VISTA), which can sensitize tumor cells

results.title: Anti-MICA antigen binding fragments, fusion molecules, cells which express and methods of using
results.abstract: Antigen binding fragments, chimeric antigen receptors, and bi-specific T-cell engagers having specificity for MICA and methods for using the same in the diagnosis and treatment of disorders associated with MICA and/or MICB expression are provided.

results.title: ANTIGEN-BINDING MOLECULE CAPABLE OF BINDING TO TWO OR MORE ANTIGEN MOLECULES REPEATEDLY
